REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT IS ENJOINED FROM ILLEGALLY SEVERING OR INTERFERING WITH PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED HOUSING SERVICES: STORAGE SPACE AND PARKING. FOR THE DURATION OF THE INJUNCTION PLAINTIFF SHALL NOT BE REQUESTED TO DECREASE THE STORAGE SPACE (PARTIES TO PRESENT TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING THE DIMENSIONS OF THE SPACE HISTORICALLY USED FOR STORAGE WITHIN THE GARAGE) OR TO REFRAIN FROM PARKING IN THE DRIVEWAY OR GARAGE, ONE OF THESE SPACES SHALL BE AT ALL TIMES EXCLUSIVELY PROVIDED TO PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF SHALL NOT BE REQUIRED TO MAKE HERSELF AVAILABLE TO MOVE THE CAR FOR SOMEONE ELSE TO BE ABLE TO PARK AND HER CAR SHALL NOT BE BLOCKED BY ANY OTHER CARS. DEFENDANT IS NOT PRECLUDED FROM ASSERTING HIS LEGAL CLAIMS CONCERNING THE DISPUTED PARKING AND STORAGE AREA. DEFENDANT MAY BRING A MOTION TO LIFT THE INJUNCTION IF AND WHEN DEFENDANT OBTAINS INFORMATION CONCERNING THE POSSIBILITY OF USING THE GARAGE FOR BOTH (1) STORAGE; AND (2) SIDE -BY-SIDE PARKING OF TWO CARS; OR (1) STORAGE AND (2) PARKING ONE CAR IN THE GARAGE AND PLAINTIFF'S CAR IN THE DRIVEWAY (HISTORICAL PARKING SPACE). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jul-24-2019 Off Calendar

Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED as follows: Defendant is enjoined from illegally severing or interfering with Plaintiff's alleged housing services: storage space and parking. For the duration of the injunction Plaintiff shall not be requested to decrease the storage space (parties to present to the Court at the hearing the dimensions of the space historically used for storage within the garage) or to refrain from parking in the driveway or garage, one of these spaces shall be at all times exclusively provided to Plaintiff and Plaintiff shall not be required to make herself available to move the car for someone else to be able to park and her car shall not be blocked by any other cars. Defendant is not precluded from asserting his legal claims concerning the disputed parking and storage area. Defendant may bring a motion to lift the injunction if and when Defendant obtains information concerning the possibility of using the garage for both (1) storage; and (2) side -by-side parking of two cars; or (1) storage and (2) parking one car in the garage and Plaintiff's car in the driveway (historical parking space). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
